frameworks.name: Bayesian latent-cause inference
frameworks.one_line: structure learning segments experience into latent causes governing memory traces

frameworks.name: classical associative learning
frameworks.one_line: trace modification mechanism extended with latent-cause-gated eligibility

frameworks.name: Pavlovian conditioning
frameworks.one_line: empirical domain used to validate the computational model

core_question: When does retrieval modify an existing memory trace versus create a new one?
key_assumptions: memory modification proceeds via classical associative learning
eligibility for modification is gated by inferred latent-cause structure
the agent segments experience into statistically distinct clusters
probabilistic / generative inference underlies trace assignment
manipulations: inferred sameness vs novelty of latent cause across sensory observations
key_findings: model decides between forming a new memory and modifying an old one based on latent-cause inference
simulations reproduce major Pavlovian memory-modification phenomena
interpretations: memory modification is governed by structure learning over latent causes, not by retrieval alone
old and new observations share a trace only when attributed to the same latent cause
